notes.content: The Asian Studies major is part of the International & Area Studies program and is designed for students who want to focus their studies on the culture and society of the following sub-regions: Asia, Southeast Asia, South Asia, and Central Asia. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for this major. To be considered for admission to this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer: One year of a college level Asian language OR At least one year of high school in which the primary language of instruction was an Asian language and at least two preparatory courses listed below (in addition to the foreign language). Completion of an intermediate/advanced foreign language is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED . All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://www.international.ucla.edu/asba and https://admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
notes.contentHtml:
The Asian Studies major is part of the International & Area Studies program and is designed for students who want to focus their studies on the culture and society of the following sub-regions: Asia, Southeast Asia, South Asia, and Central Asia.


Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for this major.


To be considered for admission to this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer:

  • One year of a college level Asian language OR
  • At least one year of high school in which the primary language of instruction was an Asian language and at least two preparatory courses listed below (in addition to the foreign language).

Completion of an intermediate/advanced foreign language is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED.

All courses must be taken for a letter grade.


For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://www.international.ucla.edu/asba and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

notes.content: Complete through the 6th quarter or two years of any modern language spoken in Asia. This may include: Chinese, Filipino, Hindi-Urdu, Indonesian, Japanese, Korean, Thai and Vietnamese.
notes.contentHtml: Complete through the 6th quarter or two years of any modern language spoken in Asia. This may include: Chinese, Filipino, Hindi-Urdu, Indonesian, Japanese, Korean, Thai and Vietnamese.

notes.content: Complete a total of 5 courses over the following areas listed below: 1) Area Studies, 2) International Politics and Markets, 3) International Societies and Cultures
notes.contentHtml: Complete a total of 5 courses over the following areas listed below: 1) Area Studies, 2) International Politics and Markets, 3) International Societies and Cultures

notes.content: The AP Exam scores listed below may be used to meet to satisfy major prep A score of 3 or higher on the AP Human Geography exam can be used in place of the cultural geography course. A score of 3 or higher on the AP World History exam can be used in place of the contemporary world history course. A score of 3 or higher on the AP Comparative Government exam can be used in place of the comparative politics course. A score of 4-5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 ( if taken before spring 2021 ) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course. A score of 5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 ( if taken after spring 2021 ) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course. A score of 4-5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 ( if taken before spring 2021 ) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course. A score of 5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 ( if taken after spring 2021 ) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course.
notes.contentHtml:
The AP Exam scores listed below may be used to meet to satisfy major prep

  • A score of 3 or higher on the AP Human Geography exam can be used in place of the cultural geography course.
  • A score of 3 or higher on the AP World History exam can be used in place of the contemporary world history course.
  • A score of 3 or higher on the AP Comparative Government exam can be used in place of the comparative politics course.
  • A score of 4-5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 (if taken before spring 2021) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course.
  • A score of 5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 (if taken after spring 2021) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course.
  • A score of 4-5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 (if taken before spring 2021) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course.
  • A score of 5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 (if taken after spring 2021) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course.
